per_memory.cml_output: F: Production Redis on redis-prod-a.internal:6380, TLS enabled; maxmemory=4GB, allkeys-lru eviction; replica=redis-prod-b.internal, async replication, lag <50ms. Backups to offsite object store nightly at 03:15 UTC. RDB disabled, AOF off ∵ keep write latency <1ms at p99.

F: Postgres cluster name db-prod-01; version 16.2; OS Ubuntu 24.04; data directory /var/lib/postgresql/16/main; WAL archiving on; archive_command to /mnt/backup/pg-wal/; shared_buffers 8GB; effective_cache_size 24GB; connection limit 200; pgbouncer on port 6432; mode transaction pooling.

D: sqlite-vec chosen over pgvector for Mnemos ∵ embedded, single-writer, zero-deployment vector search; Qdrant optional for multi-node/higher QPS needs. Decision date: 2026-02-13 during v6 planning. Alternatives considered: pgvector, Chroma, Weaviate (rejected ∵ separate server process required).

D: Switched embedding model from Ollama Qwen3 to FastEmbed multilingual-e5-large on 2026-02-15 (v7.0) ∵ Qwen3 was 4x slower on CPU and poor with Swedish, critical for production memory store. F: e5-large is 1024-dim, ONNX format, CPU-native; runs ~120 passages/sec on Ryzen 7 8-core. Qwen3 had slightly higher raw cosine on English.

P: direct, playful tone in casual replies; no em dashes in writing projects → use hyphens or parentheses; suggested text blocks wrapped in copyable code fences; direct, proactive style preferred over cautious hedging. When requesting reviews, prefers honest opinions over validation.
P: Email replies concise, 3-5 sentences, casual tone, no AI-generated footers. Internal messages: 1-2 sentences. Long-form writing for documentation/RFCs only, not routine correspondence.

F: Production DB: example-shop.com on db-prod-01; MariaDB socket: /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock; DB name: prod_shop; user: shop_prod_rhclh7. Staging DB: test_shop on same server. Binlog format: ROW; retention: 7 days; size cap: 50GB.

W: Do not run db:migrate:fresh against production; staging box test_shop has trigger prevent_fresh raising exception, production does not. F: Lost 12 hours of customer messages in earlier incident due to wrong --env flag. P: Always double-check APP_ENV before any migrate command.

F: Jina rerank-v2 cross-encoder trained on query-document pairs; returns raw logits, not calibrated probabilities. F: Sigmoid applied to logits; sigmoid(raw) >= 0.55 indicates duplicates, below is noise. F: Threshold set as DEDUP_RERANK_THRESHOLD in mnemos.constants.

F: Topic-level merge clusters (cosine 0.75 to 0.88) need more aggressive guard rails than tight near-duplicate clusters (above 0.88) ∵ LLM over-generalizes and drops specifics with loosely related inputs. F: Hierarchical binary merge in v10 mitigates this by merging two memories at a time, maintaining 'output roughly one input size' intuition.

F: Rotate qdrant API key: 1) Generate new key with `openssl rand -hex 32`; 2) Write to `/etc/qdrant/config.yaml` under `service.api_key`; 3) `systemctl restart qdrant`; 4) Update `MNEMOS_QDRANT_API_KEY` in `/etc/mnemos/env`; 5) `systemctl restart mnemos-mcp`; 6) Verify with `curl -H 'api-key: NEW' http://localhost:6333/collections`. Old key remains valid until rotation completes.

F: Mnemos LongMemEval benchmark R@5: 98.94% hybrid+rerank without CML, 99.15% with CML; 470 non-abstention questions in cleaned longmemeval_s.json, HuggingFace dataset. End-to-end QA accuracy: 77.4% with Sonnet answerer, Opus judge on 500-question set. All numbers reproducible from scripts under benchmarks/.

F: FastEmbed loads e5-large in ~9s on cold start, uses ~2.1GB RAM; inference throughput 120 passages/s on Ryzen 7 8-core with ONNX CPU runtime. Warm-up at MCP server start; first memory_search post-boot ~15ms (model preloaded).

per_memory.cml_output: F: Stripe webhook integration; issue: signature verification failures. F: Cause: FastAPI middleware read body stream before webhook handler, leaving stream empty for Stripe verification. F: Stripe requires raw request body, byte for byte. D: Fix: Disable body parsing on /webhooks/stripe route using dependency override. L: Importance of reading Stripe docs carefully. L: Documenting fixes to avoid forgetting.

D: Stick with sqlite-vec for Mnemos default configuration; pgvector as optional backend. ∵ Zero-deployment is a feature for single-user memory system.  
F: pgvector considered ∵ already running Postgres for application data; sqlite-vec rock solid, zero deployment, lives in same SQLite file.  
P: No need for multi-node vector search at current scale.

F: Reference box: Ryzen 7 8-core, 64GB ECC RAM. FastEmbed loading multilingual-e5-large: ~9s cold start; model RAM: ~2.1GB. Inference throughput: ~120 passages/s (batch of 100 short passages, CPU-native ONNX). First memory_search post-startup: ~15ms (model warm in memory via MCP server warmup hook). D: Quote numbers more carefully.

F: On 2023-01-22, ran db:migrate:fresh on example-shop.com; APP_ENV pointed to production, not staging. Lost ~12 hours of customer messages. Staging has prevent_fresh trigger to block destructive migrations; production lacks this trigger. D: Hard rule: verify APP_ENV and target hostname before running db:migrate:fresh. P: Never skip check, even if tired or rushed.

P: Casual tone preferred; playful direct style > hedged corporate-speak. No em dashes; use hyphens/parentheses/end sentence. Text blocks in copyable code fences. Email replies: concise, 3-5 sentences, >5 only if necessary. Reviews: honest opinions > validation; 'great' = insufficient feedback.
